item.title: Can Europe break free from US tech dominance? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Q&A with FT Innovation Editor John Thornhill and EU correspondent Barbara Moens, on Thursday March 26 at 1pm GMT

item.title: Big drugmakers saved at least $5bn on US taxes shifting income overseas
item.description: New disclosures show how pharma groups reduced federal levies last year by assigning profits to low-tax jurisdictions

item.title: Flagship Blackstone credit fund posts first monthly loss since 2022
item.description: Loan markdowns and market declines led to negative return for the first time in more than three years

item.title: Goldman lawyer who resigned over Epstein links paid $25mn in 2025
item.description: Kathy Ruemmler to step down as the bank’s general counsel at the end of June

item.title: US to deploy thousands more troops to the Middle East
item.description: Marines and sailors to arrive in the region in three to four weeks, signalling possible new phase in conflict

item.title: Airline industry hit by biggest crisis since pandemic
item.description: Middle East war has unleashed severe disruption for the aviation sector

item.title: BA owner IAG threatens to walk away from bid for TAP
item.description: Group has warned Portuguese government it will only bid for airline if majority ownership rule is relaxed

item.title: A bet on Bill Ackman’s new listing is complex
item.description: The hedge fund manager is seeking to extend his Pershing Square portfolio in multiple vehicles

item.title: Stockpickers: Essentra, Wickes, Trustpilot
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Directors’ Deals: IAG insiders divest ahead of war-induced sell-off
item.description: Airline group’s shares have fallen by a quarter since the attack on Iran

item.title: Iran war will scar the global economy
item.description: Scenarios that markets and policymakers hoped to avoid are now materialising

item.title: Why Wall Street is scouting college sports stars
item.description: Banks have realised the potential from tapping in to tomorrow’s elite - and by definition wealthy - athletes

item.title: How the oil shock might spread
item.description: Measures undertaken by vulnerable economies may be only the start of things to come

item.title: Rivals launch mocking ads to capitalise on Hargreaves fee backlash
item.description: The UK’s largest investment site more than tripled maximum annual charges on a range of products

item.title: Retail investors aren’t crazy for Farage and Kwarteng’s bitcoin business
item.description: Unpopular

item.title: Hedge fund Pharo sues landlord over ‘intolerable’ building works noise
item.description: Macro specialist brings lawsuit against UAE-backed landlord over disturbance at London headquarters

item.title: The UK’s meningitis outbreak and the grim legacy of Covid
item.description: What the Chemistry Club cases tell us about lessons unlearnt from the pandemic

item.title: Centerview to face trial over star banker’s pay dispute
item.description: David Handler, a technology dealmaker, wins reversal from Delaware Supreme Court

item.title: Energy Source Forum: Powering up the energy workforce
item.description: With the demand for energy growing rapidly and the complexity of the sector increasing, the need for workers will not abate

item.title: Donald Trump urges narrow AI regulation amid fierce Maga backlash
item.description: White House tries to block state-level laws but faces pressure to offer a federal rule book for the controversial technology

item.title: Two arrested after attempt to enter UK nuclear submarine base
item.description: Man and woman, one believed to be Iranian, held after approaching Faslane

item.title: Supermicro drops 28% after co-founder charged with smuggling Nvidia chips to China
item.description: US prosecutors allege two employees and a contractor conspired to illegally ship servers through south-east Asia

item.title: Goldman’s chief warns private credit risks show cycle ‘has not been repealed’
item.description: David Solomon’s comments in annual shareholder letter underscore Wall Street’s wariness around non-bank lending

item.title: Unilever in talks to sell food division to spice and sauce maker McCormick
item.description: Consumer goods giant working to offload unit worth tens of billions of dollars as it pivots to beauty and personal care

item.title: UK regulator to probe collapsed mortgage lender MFS
item.description: Investigation follows failure of Mayfair-based lender and linked companies owned by founder Paresh Raja

item.title: Iberian blackout prompts calls for Europe-wide grid reforms
item.description: Report into last year’s catastrophic outage says the problem did not lie with solar and wind power

item.title: Macquarie and Chinese fund explore sale of £1bn stake in UK gas network
item.description: Australian asset manager revives effort to sell stake in Cadent it explored two years ago

item.title: Iran war is the greatest threat to global energy ‘in history’, warns IEA
item.description: Agency’s head Fatih Birol says recovery of oil and gasfields in Gulf region could take more than six months

item.title: Big Tech’s new carbon play
item.description: Is the latest twist on offsets a good idea?

item.title: Can a surge of new arrivals rescue Hong Kong’s property sales market?
item.description: A surge of residents, many of them mainlanders, have settled in the city since 2023, driving rents to record levels. Hopes are riding high for a sales market bounceback
